title: Drug use disorder deaths by sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths from [drug use disorders](#dod:drug_use_disorder) by sex.
caption:
Drug use disorder deaths by sex

Estimated annual number of deaths from drug use disorders by sex.

This stacked area chart tracks the estimated annual number of global deaths from drug use disorders from 2000 to 2021, separated by sex. The vertical axis measures the number of deaths, ranging from 0 to 180,000, while the horizontal axis marks the years. The data is divided into two distinct bands: a dark purple area at the bottom representing males and a lighter purple area stacked on top representing females. In the year 2000, the total number of deaths sits just below 80,000, with the male portion accounting for approximately 60,000 and the female portion making up the remaining 20,000. Between 2000 and 2012, the total count remains relatively stable, fluctuating slightly within the 80,000 range. Starting around 2013, both bands begin a visible upward trend. By 2015, the total reaches approximately 105,000. The growth accelerates significantly after 2018. In 2021, the chart shows the highest values in the series, with the total number of deaths reaching approximately 175,000. At this endpoint, the male band extends to roughly 125,000, while the female band adds another 50,000 to the total. Throughout the entire twenty-one-year period, the dark purple area representing males consistently occupies a larger share of the total height than the lighter purple area representing females.
